channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mass evacuation of Philippine villages underway after a brief but major volcanic eruption
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — About 87,000 people were being evacuated in a central Philippine region Tuesday a day after a volcano briefly erupted with a towering ash plume and superhot streams of gas and debris hurtling down its western slopes.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ukrainian man fleeing war rescued with his kitten on a perilous journey through Romanian mountains
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A Ukrainian man who embarked on a perilous journey fleeing his war-torn country into Romania was rescued from a deep mountain ravine in subzero temperatures with an unlikely companion: his months-old kitten named Peach.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A gang leader in Haiti is accused of massacring older people to avenge his son's death
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A gang leader who controls a key port in Haiti's capital is accused of massacring older people and Vodou religious leaders in his community to avenge his son's death, according to the government and human rights organizations that estimate more than 100 were killed.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick for intel chief, faces questions on Capitol Hill amid Syria fallout
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, Tulsi Gabbard, is making her case on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden creates Native American boarding school national monument to mark era of forced assimilation
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President Joe Biden designated a national monument at a former Native American boarding school in Pennsylvania on Monday to honor the resilience of Indigenous tribes whose children were forced to attend the school and hundreds of similar abusive institutions.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Petition urges food allergy measures after Disney influencer's death
item.description: The petition calls on California lawmakers to institute new laws, including: a law that would make EpiPens a mandatory part of the emergency supplies at all restaurants or venues that serve food.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO was likely killed with a ghost gun that can be made at home
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The brazen killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO was likely carried out with a ghost gun, one of the nearly untraceable weapons that can be made a home, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Petition urges food allergy measures after Disney influencer's death
item.description: A Change.org petition calling on more food allergy measures in California has been launched after influencer Dominique Brown passed away after experiencing an allergic reaction to holiday food served at an event in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against Karen Read supporter who scattered rubber ducks and fake $100 bills
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Massachusetts judge dismissed criminal charges Monday against a backer of Karen Read who admitted placing dozens of yellow rubber ducks and fake $100 bills around town in support of Read. Richard Schiffer Jr.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to watch 'Monday Night Football' in 'The Simpsons' universe
item.description: The prime-time matchup between the Cincinnati Bengals and Dallas Cowboys will also take place at Springfield’s Atoms Stadium as part of “The Simpsons Funday Football” alternate broadcast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Check your junk mail: Some affected by the Equifax data breach to get more money
item.description: Roughly 147.9 million Americans were affected by the breach, which compromised users’ names, Social Security numbers, birthdays, driver’s license numbers.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s vow to end birthright citizenship could face legal challenges, GOP skeptics
item.description: President-elect Trump has doubled down on his vow to end birthright citizenship, a task that would likely face legal challenges as well as skepticism from within his own party. For starters, the 14th Amendment grants citizenship to those born in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado woman to deliver clean water filters to African villages
item.description: What began with just the filters has evolved, every trip back, with more things like mosquito nets, tarps and now solar-powered lanterns.
item.dc:creator: Nate Belt, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon offering $25 flights for Prime members — but only for one eligible group
item.description: The deal, which runs through Friday, aims to offer 1,000 bookings per day for a total of 5,000 $25 fares.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tren de Aragua fugitive arrested in Tennessee
item.description: Tren de Aragua member Luis Alejandro Ruiz Godoy was arrested on a litany of charges including terrorism and human trafficking, per Interpol.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to Hawaii handgun license law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a defendant’s Second Amendment challenge to Hawaii laws that make it a crime to carry handguns or ammunition in public without a license.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Duck rescued from lake after getting tangled in fishing line, lure
item.description: Colorado deputies are asking people to correctly dispose of fishing lines and hooks after authorities waded through cold waters to rescue a tangled young bird.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sebastian Rogers: Missing teen's dad has renewed faith in investigation
item.description: On Sebastian Rogers' 16th birthday, the missing Sumner County boy's father said a recent meeting with law enforcement has restored his confidence in their case.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mayer, NewsNation

item.title: Gas prices drop to lowest in 3 years, AAA says
item.description: Good news for the millions of drivers on American roads: gas prices are falling. The national average for a gallon of gas has hit a three-year low, the American Automobile Association (AAA) recently announced.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, WGN 9

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Mysterious drone sightings continue to be reported in New Jersey without answers
item.description: Mysterious drones continue to be reported flying overhead in New Jersey, as residents remain puzzled as to why there has been an uptick in drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dodgers reach $17M, 1-year deal with Conforto and $22M, 2-year contract with Treinen, AP sources say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers agreed to a $17 million, one-year contract to add outfielder Michael Conforto and a $22 million, two-year deal to retain right-hander Blake Treinen, people familiar with the negotiations told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump names several picks for State Department roles
item.description: In several posts on Truth Social, Trump shared the new positions to work under his nominated Secretary of State Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: Cracker Barrel has issued an apology after a Maryland school district said a group of students with disabilities was denied service at one of the chain's restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's 'Meet the Press' interview
item.description: President-elect Trump's first sit-down interview since winning a second White House term aired Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Big Ten wins playoff selection derby, followed by SEC despite notable Alabama omission
item.description: College football's conference shakeup left concerns about two super conferences dominating the playoff field. They weren't totally unfounded or 100% borne out. The Big Ten, not the Southeastern Conference, was the biggest winner Sunday. The ACC scored, too.
item.dc:creator: JOHN ZENOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US strikes dozens of ISIS camps and operatives in Syria amid Assad's downfall
item.description: U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) announced Sunday it conducted air strikes against ISIS targets and operatives in Syria amid the downfall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giant statue of the Buddha in New Jersey becomes interfaith hub and spiritual home for many
item.description: FRANKLIN TOWNSHIP, N.J. (AP) — Just off a state highway in New Jersey, one of the largest statues of the Buddha in the United States appears unexpectedly in the middle of a backyard.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill aims to combat "flippers" who quickly buy up the limited supply of highly sought-after spirits in Ohio to illegally resell them on a secondary market for significantly more than what they paid.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, WGN 9

item.title: Columbia group's antisemitic newspaper draws outrage from NY lawmaker, as university investigates
item.description: Students at Columbia University launched an anti-Israel paper that was distributed on campus last week, the club announced on social media with images.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame reopens its doors to Macron and other world leaders in a rare symbol of unity
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, KTLA 5

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds use of race in Naval Academy admissions, saying a diverse military is stronger
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge on Friday ruled that the U.S. Naval Academy can continue considering race in its admissions process, finding that military cohesion and other national security factors mean the school should not be subjected to the same standards as civilian universities.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews recover the body of a woman from a Pennsylvania sinkhole after a 4-day search
item.description: The remains of a woman who fell into a sinkhole were recovered Friday, four days after she went missing while searching for her cat, a state police spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials release bodycam video of police shooting that killed 15-year-old Ohio boy
item.description: AKRON, Ohio (AP) — The mayor and police chief in an Ohio city where a 15-year-old boy was fatally shot by police on Thanksgiving night said bodycam video of the shooting raises questions that must be addressed. The boy's family said Friday that they want answers, too.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA requires bulk testing of raw milk supply for bird flu virus
item.description: The nation’s milk supply must be tested for the bird flu virus H5N1 under a new order announced Friday by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) – here's why.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, ABC 4
